old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-hvxx","title":"Persist Rust link tuples across restart","description":"Rust Stack::new_default_epoch always starts at (128,0), and public SecureStack construction uses it. Restarting under the same identity can reuse signed link tuples. Require caller-provided persisted next tuple/exhaustion state or key rotation when state is unavailable; remove unsafe production default and add restart tests.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-19T00:18:49.706941Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-19T00:18:49.706941Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-hvxx","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-e60z","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-07-19T00:18:49.837805Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

new_value: Updated Stack::new to take persisted seq (stack.rs:155, seqnum: LinkSeqNum::new(seq)); removed unsafe new_default_epoch; updated SecureStack::from_radio and all tests to provide explicit tuple state preventing reuse on restart (secure.rs:90, stack.rs test, secure test). Added implicit support for persisted next tuple.
